title: Ooidonk Castle

infobox.value: ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Kasteel Ooidonk*) is a [castle](/wiki/Castle "Castle") in the city of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze"), [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). The castle is the residence of the current Count [t'Kint de Roodenbeke](/wiki/House_of_T%27Kint_de_Roodenbeke "House of T'Kint de Roodenbeke"). Ooidonk Castle is situated at an [altitude of 11](http://elevationmap.net/ooidonkdreef-9-9800-deinze-belgium?latlngs=%2851.00146524833842,3.5838469533729267%29) meters.

Creve, Piet; Maes, Daphné (1970-01-01). ["Project onder de loep. Vogels die niet vliegen gaan dood. 30 jaar Ghanezen in Gent en Oost-Vlaanderen"](https://doi.org/10.21825%2Fbr.v20i3.7943). *Brood & Rozen*. **20** (3). [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.21825/br.v20i3.7943](https://doi.org/10.21825%2Fbr.v20i3.7943). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Castle in East Flanders, Belgium

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8b/Ooidonk_castle_view.JPG/250px-Ooidonk_castle_view.JPG)](/wiki/File:Ooidonk_castle_view.JPG)

Ooidonk Castle from the rear

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/76/Ooidonk_castle_detail.JPG/250px-Ooidonk_castle_detail.JPG)](/wiki/File:Ooidonk_castle_detail.JPG)

Ooidonk Castle from the left

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d1/Ooidonk_castle%2C_Belgium_%3B_Ferraris_Map.jpg/250px-Ooidonk_castle%2C_Belgium_%3B_Ferraris_Map.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ooidonk_castle,_Belgium_;_Ferraris_Map.jpg)

Ooidonk Castle on the [Ferraris map](/wiki/Ferraris_map "Ferraris map") (around 1775)

**Ooidonk Castle** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Kasteel Ooidonk*) is a [castle](/wiki/Castle "Castle") in the city of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze"), [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). The castle is the residence of the current Count [t'Kint de Roodenbeke](/wiki/House_of_T%27Kint_de_Roodenbeke "House of T'Kint de Roodenbeke"). Ooidonk Castle is situated at an [altitude of 11](http://elevationmap.net/ooidonkdreef-9-9800-deinze-belgium?latlngs=%2851.00146524833842,3.5838469533729267%29) meters.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ooidonk_Castle&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Ooidonk is already mentioned in 1230 : "Nicolas, chaplain of Hodunc".[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

A fortress was first built on the site of the current castle, intended to defend the city of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") and to fortify the river [Leie](/wiki/Leie "Leie"). This fortress was owned by Jean de Fosseux, lord of [Nevele](/wiki/Nevele "Nevele").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The castle was destroyed for the first time in 1491 by the people of Ghent, in revolt against [Maximilian I](/wiki/Maximilian_I,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Maximilian I, Holy Roman Emperor").

In 1568, [Philippe II de Montmorency-Nivelle](/wiki/Philip_de_Montmorency,_Count_of_Horn "Philip de Montmorency, Count of Horn"), Count of Hornes, Lord of Nevele and owner of the estate was beheaded, along with the [Count of Egmont](/wiki/Lamoral,_Count_of_Egmont "Lamoral, Count of Egmont"), by the Spanish [Duke of Alba](/wiki/Duke_of_Alba "Duke of Alba") in Brussels.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 1579, during the [wars of religion](/wiki/French_Wars_of_Religion "French Wars of Religion"), the castle, defenseless, was destroyed by the Ghent [Calvinists](/wiki/Calvinism "Calvinism").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The ruins and lands were later acquired by [Martin della Faille](/wiki/Martin_della_Faille "Martin della Faille"), a merchant from Antwerp. He rebuilt the castle in a [Renaissance](/wiki/Renaissance_architecture "Renaissance architecture") style and transformed it into a place of residence in [Hispano-Flemish style](/wiki/Hispano-Flemish_style "Hispano-Flemish style").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 1864, the castle and lands were acquired by [Henri t' Kint de Rodenbeke](/wiki/House_of_t%27Kint_de_Roodenbeke "House of t'Kint de Roodenbeke") who went on to become [President of the Senate](/wiki/List_of_presidents_of_the_Senate_(Belgium) "List of presidents of the Senate (Belgium)") and [Minister of State](/wiki/Minister_of_State_(Belgium) "Minister of State (Belgium)"). He restored and transformed the castle.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

It is now the private property of Count Henry [t'Kint de Roodenbeke](/wiki/T%27Kint_de_Roodenbeke "T'Kint de Roodenbeke"), son of the late Count Juan [t'Kint de Roodenbeke](/wiki/T%27Kint_de_Roodenbeke "T'Kint de Roodenbeke").

In 1944, the castle was classified as a protected landmark. In 1980, other constructions around it were also classified, and in 1995, so was the rest of the estate. Ooidonk Castle is open to the public from April 1 to September 15, and the park (with its French garden) can be visited year round.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
